response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled polyglot engineer with genuine interest in developer tooling, but critically lacks the core qualifications for this role. Java appears only briefly in supporting contexts and Kotlin is entirely absent from the resume. There is no evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework experience across a 15+ year career. His closest relevant work — writing C/C++ components for NI LabWindows (a C IDE) and building test tooling for the D language — demonstrates tooling aptitude but does not transfer to JVM IDE internals or enterprise Java development.
strengths: Genuine developer-tooling orientation: built test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial) and an extensible test runner for D, and wrote IDE components at National Instruments
Proven experience with high-throughput, complex systems (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP requests/second with low-latency async I/O)
Strong communication and community presence: organized Refactoring Dojos, Code Katas, and delivered multi-day technical workshops
concerns: No substantive Java or Kotlin expertise — listed in the skills section but absent from any meaningful work history; Kotlin is not mentioned at all
Zero demonstrated experience with Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework across the entire career
IDE internals experience is limited to C/C++ for a non-JVM product; no IntelliJ Platform, Eclipse plugin, bytecode manipulation, or JVM tooling work is evident
